page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9ae.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, and it operates with a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage solutions for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance without resource contention, making it a stable choice for workloads requiring consistent compute power. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose computing.

The ecs.g9ae.4xlarge is designed for workloads that require a balance of compute and memory resources. Suitable use cases include web serving, application servers, enterprise applications, containerized microservices, and moderate database hosting where dedicated CPU performance is necessary.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9ae.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at 2.25 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features a single thread per core design to deliver predictable compute performance. It operates on the KVM hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options. With a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, the ecs.g9ae.4xlarge is optimized for workloads requiring stable, non-shared CPU resources, such as web servers, application hosting, containerized microservices, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
